feedback.comment: It's got more recently listened to tracks, there are some unexpected results in there (eg. Elmiene — Useless (Without You)) , but on a whole - they seem quite relevant to recent tracks I've added/liked/listened to.

results.title: Golden
results.album: KPop Demon Hunters (Soundtrack from the Netflix Film)

results.title: All I Want for Christmas Is You

feedback.comment: This seems to be what the 'popular' opinion of others might be - but doesn't match my specific tastes. There are artists/tracks in there I  like - but it feels very out of align with my 'ideal' list.
This has a good mix of things I've liked/lsitend to recently. It doesn't have a lot of novelty - but doesn't feel like a safe and good list of tracks.

results.title: t h e . c l i m b . b a c k
Glenwood
results.album: I Feel The Everblack Festering Within Me

feedback.comment: This seems to be more diverse in style/tone than others - bringing some older classics in here (eg. Tender — More or Less). There are some tracks I'm not even sure what they are though (Yan Silvestre — Atrás do Pataco), so I'm confused on what that's doing in there.

results.title: t h e . c l i m b . b a c k

feedback.comment: This feels like the 'things I could listen to all day' - that set of tracks for any mood/time of the day. It doesn't really push the boundaries of novelty, style, and it's a bit too skewed to softer styles (all my metal is pretty much gone) - but I could jam to this.

results.title: Glenwood
results.album: I Feel The Everblack Festering Within Me

results.title: Fear and Loathing in Los Angeles
Whiplash
results.album: The Sky, The Earth & All Between

feedback.comment: Strong overall list, good diversity - mixing some bangers from different styles (eg. Caskey — Fear and Loathing in Los Angeles alongside Korn — Freak On a Leash)> It does 'group' tracks from the same artist that I'd like to spread out more (eg. ERRA and Sleep Token), but the mix overall is good.

Digging the tracks here a lot - but there is some duplication of artists next to each other (Eg. Pantera), and there could be more novelty from older/other styles. But I would enjoy this list all the same.
This doesn't feel any different thank #7 - same commetary applies.

results.title: Fear and Loathing in Los Angeles
Whiplash
results.album: The Sky, The Earth & All Between

results.title: No Limit (feat. A$AP Rocky & Cardi B)

feedback.comment: I like the diversity of styles of content, but it doesn't always hit on the tracks I would pick (eg. G-Eazy — No Limit (feat. A$AP Rocky & Cardi B) and Big Sean — I Don't Fuck With You I wouldn't want to listen to)
Good mix of new/old - strong diversity other than tiny bits of duplication (eg. Pantera). Could use more style diversity of softer styles and harder styles though.
